Retrieval embeddings with EmbeddingGemma

Source: vignettes/embeddinggemma.Rmd
embeddinggemma.Rmd
library(Rbebelm)
weights_file <- Sys.getenv(
  "EMBEDDING_GEMMA_WEIGHTS_FILE",
  "/root/bebelm/embeddinggemma-300M-Q8_0.gguf"
)
stopifnot(file.exists(weights_file))
model <- embeddinggemma_model_load(weights_file, num_threads = 2)

A separate retrieval model

BebelModel and EmbeddingGemmaModel deliberately have different APIs. LFM2.5 is a causal generator whose intermediate states are exposed only as contextual features. EmbeddingGemma is a retrieval-trained, bidirectional text encoder whose final vectors are intended for cosine similarity, retrieval, clustering, and related representation tasks.

The package’s EmbeddingGemma path is implemented in Rust. It reads the GGUF and its tokenizer metadata directly, then runs:

  1. BOS/content/EOS SentencePiece tokenization;
  2. 24 bidirectional Gemma layers, using five symmetric-window layers followed by one global-attention layer;
  3. final RMSNorm and attention-mask mean pooling;
  4. the learned 768-to-3072 and 3072-to-768 projections; and
  5. optional Matryoshka truncation followed by L2 normalization.

No llama.cpp, PyTorch, ONNX Runtime, or SentencePiece C++ library is linked into the package. Oracle tests use the pinned ggml-org Q8_0 artifact at revision 0f741b5a6585bd53aeb15cd1372c56f2a0f65e12 (SHA-256 b5ce9d77a3fc4b3b39ccb5643c36777911cc4eb46a66962eadfa3f5f60490d63).

Query and document prompts

EmbeddingGemma was trained with asymmetric retrieval prompts. Use the dedicated helpers rather than embedding unformatted query and document strings.

query <- embeddinggemma_embed_query(model, "Which city is the capital of Mali?")
passages <- c(
  mali = "Bamako is the capital and largest city of Mali.",
  italy = "Rome is the capital city of Italy.",
  desert = "The Sahara is a desert in northern Africa."
)
documents <- embeddinggemma_embed_document(model, passages)

scores <- drop(documents %*% as.numeric(query))
sort(scores, decreasing = TRUE)
##      mali     italy    desert 
## 0.6511533 0.2656509 0.1596326

Character-vector inputs are packed into bounded encoder batches to reuse model matrix reads. Position and attention boundaries are reset for every element, so a batched result is numerically equivalent to encoding each string separately.

Document titles are optional. NULL applies the model’s literal title: none prompt; a title scalar is recycled over the input vector.

with_titles <- embeddinggemma_embed_document(
  model,
  passages[c("mali", "italy")],
  title = c("Mali", "Italy")
)
with_titles
## <EmbeddingGemma embeddings>
##   shape: 2 x 768
##   task: retrieval_document
##   normalized: yes

Other tasks and Matryoshka dimensions

The generic function requires an explicit task so it cannot silently mix retrieval-document, retrieval-query, or semantic-similarity prompts.

similarity_embeddings <- embeddinggemma_embed(
  model,
  c("A pleasant day", "Beautiful weather"),
  task = "semantic_similarity",
  dimensions = 256L
)

similarity_embeddings %*% t(similarity_embeddings)
##           [,1]      [,2]
## [1,] 1.0000000 0.8796266
## [2,] 0.8796266 1.0000000
attr(similarity_embeddings, "embedding_info")
## $model
## [1] "EmbeddingGemma-300M"
## 
## $architecture
## [1] "gemma-embedding"
## 
## $source_task
## [1] "contrastive text representation learning"
## 
## $task
## [1] "semantic_similarity"
## 
## $prompt_applied
## [1] TRUE
## 
## $pooling
## [1] "attention-mask mean"
## 
## $projection
## [1]  768 3072  768
## 
## $dimensions
## [1] 256
## 
## $l2_normalized
## [1] TRUE
## 
## $retrieval_trained
## [1] TRUE
## 
## $context_length
## [1] 2048
## 
## $token_count
## [1] 12 11
## 
## $truncated
## [1] FALSE FALSE

Supported dimensions are 768, 512, 256, and 128. Lower-dimensional outputs are the leading dimensions of the learned 768-vector and are re-normalized. Index and query vectors must always use the same task contract, model artifact, dimension, and normalization setting.

Truncation and provenance

The model context is 2048 tokens including BOS and EOS. By default, longer inputs are truncated while preserving those special tokens; set truncate = FALSE to fail instead. Every result records per-input token counts and truncation flags in embedding_info.

The package does not distribute model weights. EmbeddingGemma weights remain subject to the Gemma Terms of Use and Gemma Prohibited Use Policy.
